title: EEG: Improvisation and Musical Structures

funding_references.funder_name: Presidential Scholars in Society and Neuroscience program at Columbia University

funding_references.award_title: Presidential Scholars in Society and Neuroscience program

description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from musicians listening to chord progressions with varying harmonic structures. Participants were presented with standard and deviant musical sequences to investigate how improvisation experience influences the neural processing and categorization of musical structures. The study employs an oddball paradigm with harmonic deviants differing in either chord inversion (exemplar deviants) or harmonic function (function deviants) to probe musicians' implicit knowledge of tonal music.
methods_description: EEG data were collected while musicians listened to chord progressions consisting of three chords presented sequentially (400 ms each) followed by 400 ms silence (total trial duration 1600 ms). Four chord progression types were used as standards (ii-IV-I, ii-V-I, ii-IV6-I, ii-V6-I) in separate experimental blocks. Deviant trials (15% probability) differed in the middle chord, either as exemplar deviants (chord inversion) or function deviants (different harmonic function). Each subject completed 8 blocks with 180 trials per block, totaling 1440 trials (222 functional and 218 exemplar deviants).
